name: Turkey Chili
steps: Brown turkey with onion and pepper, about 6-8 min.
Add beans, tomatoes, and chili spices. Simmer 20 min.
Top with shredded cheddar to serve.
steps_detailed: Heat olive oil in a heavy pot over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper, cook 5-6 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Push vegetables aside, add turkey to the cleared space, and brown it, breaking it apart, 5-6 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and spice blend, cooking 1 minute to toast the spices — this blooms their flavor rather than leaving them tasting raw.
Add the beans, diced tomatoes, broth, and bay leaf. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened to your liking. Add a splash more broth if it gets too thick.
Remove the bay leaf, taste and adjust salt, and top with cheddar cheese to serve.

name: Steak & Roasted Vegetables
steps: Roast sweet potato and zucchini at 400F, about 20 min.
Sear steak 3-4 min per side to medium, rest 5 min before slicing.
Plate steak with the roasted vegetables.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 425F. Toss the sweet potato with half the oil, spread on a baking sheet, and roast 15 minutes.
Add the zucchini to the sheet, toss with a little more oil, and roast another 12-15 minutes until both are tender and lightly browned.

Meanwhile, pat the steak dry and season generously with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Let it sit at room temperature for 10-15 minutes before cooking — a cold steak straight from the fridge cooks unevenly.
Heat a heavy skillet over high heat until very hot. Sear the steak 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, or longer to your preference.
Rest the steak on a cutting board for 5 minutes before slicing against the grain — this keeps it from being tough and lets the juices redistribute instead of running out.
Sprinkle the vegetables with fresh rosemary and serve alongside the sliced steak.

name: Turkey Meatballs with Whole Wheat Spaghetti
steps: Mix turkey with breadcrumbs and egg, form into meatballs, bake at 400F for 18-20 min.
Cook spaghetti per package directions.
Warm pasta sauce, add meatballs to simmer 5 min.
Serve over spaghetti with parmesan.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F and line a baking sheet.
Combine turkey, breadcrumbs, egg, 1 tablespoon of the parmesan, garlic, and oregano in a bowl. Mix gently with your hands just until combined — overmixing makes tough meatballs.
Roll into balls about 1.5 inches across (you should get 8-10) and place on the baking sheet.
Bake 15-18 minutes until browned and cooked through (165F internal temperature).
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti per package directions.
Warm the pasta sauce in a saucepan, then add the cooked meatballs to the sauce and simmer 3-4 minutes to let them soak up flavor.
Serve over the spaghetti, topped with remaining parmesan and fresh basil.

name: Grilled Chicken with Quinoa & Zucchini
steps: Cook quinoa per package directions.
Season chicken and grill or pan-sear 5-6 min per side until cooked through.
Saute or roast zucchini until tender.
Slice chicken and plate over quinoa with zucchini.
steps_detailed: Rinse the quinoa under cold water (this removes its natural bitter coating), then cook per package directions.
Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Let sit at room temperature 10 minutes.
Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high with half the olive oil. Cook the chicken 5-6 minutes per side until it reaches 165F internally. Rest 5 minutes before slicing.
In the same pan, add the remaining oil and cook the zucchini 5-6 minutes until tender with light char marks.
Fluff the quinoa with a fork, stir in the lemon juice and parsley.
Slice the chicken and serve over the quinoa with the zucchini alongside.

name: Cottage Cheese & Fruit Bowl
steps: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with berries, almonds, and a drizzle of honey.
steps_detailed: Spoon the cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with the thawed berries and their juices, sliced almonds, and a drizzle of honey.
Finish with a light dusting of cinnamon.

name: Turkey & Cheddar Grain Bowl
steps: Combine cooled quinoa, turkey, cheese, and vegetables in a jar or container.
Refrigerate; eat cold or reheated.
steps_detailed: Cook the quinoa per package directions.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the turkey with garlic powder, 5-6 minutes.
Add the bell pepper and zucchini, cooking another 4-5 minutes until softened.
Serve the turkey and vegetables over the quinoa, topped with cheddar.

name: Baked Mac and Cheese with Chicken
steps: Cook macaroni per package directions.
Melt butter, whisk in milk and cheese over low heat until smooth.
Fold in macaroni and chicken, transfer to a baking dish.
Bake at 375F for 15 min until bubbly on top.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 375F. Cook the pasta 1-2 minutes short of package directions.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook 1 minute, stirring constantly — this raw-flour taste needs to cook off before the milk goes in.
Slowly whisk in the milk, a little at a time, to avoid lumps. Cook, whisking often, until thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 4-5 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the cheese until melted and smooth.
Fold in the pasta and diced chicken.
Transfer to a baking dish, top with panko and a dusting of paprika.
Bake 15-18 minutes until bubbling and golden on top.

name: Sunday Pot Roast
steps: Sear beef on all sides in a hot pot, 4-5 min total.
Add broth, potato, carrot, and onion.
Cover and braise on low (stovetop or 325F oven) for 2-2.5 hours until fork-tender.

ingredients_full.note: or a chuck roast cut if available

steps_detailed: Pat the meat dry and season generously with salt and pepper.

Heat olive oil in a heavy pot over medium-high heat. Sear the meat on all sides until deeply browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. This crust is where most of the roast's flavor comes from — don't rush it.
Remove the meat and add the onion, carrot, and garlic to the same pot, cooking 4-5 minutes to soften slightly and pick up the browned bits.
Stir in the tomato paste, cook 1 minute, then add the broth, thyme, and bay leaf, scraping up anything stuck to the bottom.
Return the meat to the pot along with the potatoes. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and braise on low heat (or in a 300F oven) for 2.5-3 hours, until the meat is fork-tender and shreds easily.
Remove the bay leaf, slice or shred the meat, and serve with the vegetables and pan juices spooned over.

name: Chicken Pot Pie Skillet

ingredients.note: slice, torn into pieces on top

steps: Saute chicken and carrot in butter until chicken is cooked through, 6-7 min.
Add peas and milk, simmer 4-5 min until slightly thickened.
Top with torn pie crust pieces, broil 3-4 min until golden.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F.
Melt butter in an oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Add the carrot, celery, and onion, cooking 6-7 minutes until softened.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir to coat, cooking 1 minute to remove the raw flour taste.
Slowly whisk in the broth, then the milk, stirring until smooth. Simmer 3-4 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the chicken, peas, and thyme, and season with salt and pepper.
Lay the pie crust over the top of the skillet, tucking the edges in or trimming as needed, and cut a few small slits to vent steam.
Bake 20-25 minutes until the crust is deeply golden and the filling is bubbling around the edges. Let rest 5 minutes before serving — it's molten straight out of the oven.

name: Classic Meatloaf
steps: Mix beef, breadcrumbs, egg, and ketchup; form into a loaf.
Top with a thin layer of ketchup, bake at 375F for 40-45 min.
Rest 5 min before slicing.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 350F.
Combine the beef, breadcrumbs, egg, onion, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, and half the ketchup in a bowl. Mix with your hands just until combined — overworking the meat makes a dense, tough loaf.
Shape into a loaf on a lined baking sheet or in a loaf pan.
Whisk the remaining ketchup with the brown sugar and brush half over the top of the loaf.
Bake 45 minutes, then brush with the remaining glaze and bake another 15-20 minutes, until the internal temperature reads 160F.
Rest 10 minutes before slicing — this lets the juices redistribute so it doesn't fall apart when cut.

name: Mashed Potatoes
steps: Boil potatoes until fork-tender, about 15 min.
Drain and mash with butter and milk.
steps_detailed: Place the potatoes in a pot and cover with cold water by an inch — starting them in cold water helps them cook evenly.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook 15-18 minutes until a fork slides in easily.
Drain well and return the potatoes to the hot pot for a minute to steam off excess moisture — this keeps them from turning gluey.
Mash with the butter and garlic powder, then gradually stir in the warm milk until you reach the texture you like. Warm milk incorporates more smoothly than cold, which can make the mash gummy.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

name: Baked Cod
steps: Season cod, drizzle with olive oil.
Bake at 400F for 12-15 min until it flakes easily.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F. Pat the cod dry — this helps it bake instead of steam.
Place on a lined baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, and season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Bake 12-15 minutes, until the fish flakes easily with a fork and is opaque all the way through.
Finish with a scattering of fresh parsley.

name: Roasted Sweet Potato & Broccoli
steps: Toss sweet potato and broccoli with olive oil.
Roast at 400F for 20-25 min, tossing halfway.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 425F.
Toss the sweet potato with half the oil and spread on a baking sheet. Roast 10 minutes (sweet potato needs a head start since it takes longer than broccoli).
Add the broccoli, toss with remaining oil and garlic powder, and return to the oven.
Roast another 12-15 minutes, until the sweet potato is tender and the broccoli has crisp, browned edges.

name: Grilled Chicken Breast (Plain)
steps: Season chicken with salt and pepper, rub with olive oil.
Grill or pan-sear 5-6 min per side until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Pound the chicken to an even thickness — this is the single biggest factor in avoiding a dry, overcooked outside with a raw center.
Rub with olive oil and season both sides with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Cook 5-6 minutes per side, until it reaches 165F internally and the juices run clear.
Rest 5 minutes before slicing.

name: Egg White Power Scramble
steps: Wilt spinach in a lightly oiled pan.
Add egg whites, scramble until just set.
Fold in cottage cheese off the heat.
steps_detailed: Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add the spinach and cook 1-2 minutes until wilted.
Pour in the egg whites and garlic powder. Let sit 15 seconds, then gently fold as they cook, about 2-3 minutes until just set.
Fold in the cottage cheese off heat — the residual warmth softens it slightly without turning it watery.

name: Turkey & Egg White Skillet
steps: Brown turkey with bell pepper, 6-7 min.
Pour in egg whites, scramble until set.
steps_detailed: Heat a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the turkey, breaking it apart, 4-5 minutes.
Add the bell pepper and onion, cooking another 4 minutes until softened.
Pour in the egg whites and garlic powder, folding gently as they set, about 2-3 minutes.

name: Chicken & Cottage Cheese Power Bowl
steps: Combine chicken, cottage cheese, tomato, and cucumber in a bowl.
Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon if desired.
steps_detailed: Arrange the cottage cheese in a bowl.
Top with the chicken, tomatoes, and cucumber.
Drizzle with olive oil and finish with a crack of black pepper.

name: Tuna Protein Bowl
steps: Mix everything together. Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.
steps_detailed: Mix the tuna, celery, yogurt, and lemon juice together in a bowl.
Spoon over the cottage cheese and finish with black pepper.

name: Cobb Salad
steps: Arrange chicken, bacon, egg, and blue cheese over lettuce in rows.
Toss with your dressing of choice before eating.
steps_detailed: Arrange the chopped lettuce as the base of a large plate.
Arrange the chicken, bacon, egg, blue cheese, tomatoes, and avocado in neat rows across the top — this is the classic Cobb presentation, though tossing it together works just as well.
Whisk the vinegar and oil together and drizzle over, or serve on the side.

name: Caesar Salad with Grilled Chicken
steps: Toss romaine with dressing and parmesan.
Top with sliced grilled chicken.
steps_detailed: Toast the panko in a dry skillet over medium heat, stirring often, 2-3 minutes until golden — this gives a quick crouton-like crunch without needing actual bread cubes.
Toss the romaine with the dressing and half the parmesan.
Top with the sliced chicken, remaining parmesan, toasted panko, and a crack of black pepper.

name: Waldorf Fruit Salad
steps: Toss apple, grapes, and walnuts with yogurt.

ingredients_full.note: to keep the apple from browning

steps_detailed: Toss the diced apple with lemon juice right away to keep it from browning.
Combine with the grapes, celery, and walnuts.
Whisk the yogurt and honey together, then fold through the fruit mixture until coated.

name: Cheddar Broccoli Soup
steps: Simmer broccoli in broth 10 min until tender.
Blend partially, stir in milk and cheddar until melted.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter in a pot over medium heat. Add the onion and cook 3-4 minutes until softened.
Whisk in the flour and cook 1 minute to remove the raw taste.
Slowly whisk in the milk and broth, a little at a time to avoid lumps.
Add the broccoli and garlic powder. Simmer 12-15 minutes until the broccoli is very tender.
Blend partially with an immersion blender for a chunky-smooth texture, or leave as-is for a chunkier soup.
Stir in the cheddar off heat until melted — adding cheese off direct heat helps keep the sauce smooth instead of grainy.

name: Classic Club Sandwich
steps: Layer turkey, bacon, and lettuce between toasted bread slices.
Cut into triangles.
steps_detailed: Toast the bread and let it cool slightly (spreading condiments on hot toast can make them run).
Spread mustard on one side of each slice.
Layer turkey and lettuce on the first slice, top with the second slice, then bacon and tomato, and finish with the third slice.
Secure with toothpicks and cut into quarters on a diagonal — the classic club shape.

name: Reuben Sandwich
steps: Layer corned beef, sauerkraut, and swiss between rye slices with dressing.
Grill in a pan 3-4 min per side until melted and toasted.
steps_detailed: Spread dressing on the inside of both bread slices.
Layer the corned beef, well-drained sauerkraut, and swiss cheese between the slices — draining the sauerkraut well matters, or it makes the sandwich soggy.
Butter the outside of both slices.
Grill in a skillet over medium heat, 3-4 minutes per side, pressing down gently, until the bread is golden and the cheese is fully melted.

name: Classic Tomato Soup
steps: Simmer tomatoes and onion 15 minutes, blend until smooth.
Stir in milk and warm through, don't boil.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter in a pot over medium heat. Cook the onion 5-6 minutes until softened, then add garlic and tomato paste, cooking 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes and broth. Simmer 15-20 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Blend until smooth using an immersion blender, or carefully in a stand blender in batches.
Return to low heat and stir in the cream. Warm through gently without boiling, which can cause the cream to separate.
Top with fresh basil before serving.

name: Chicken Noodle Soup
steps: Simmer broth, carrot, and noodles 10 minutes until noodles are tender.
Stir in shredded chicken and warm through.
steps_detailed: Heat a splash of oil in a pot over medium heat. Cook the onion, carrot, and celery 6-7 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, cooking 30 seconds, then add the broth and bay leaf. Bring to a simmer.
Add the egg noodles and cook per package timing, usually 7-9 minutes, until tender.
Stir in the shredded chicken and warm through, 2-3 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf, top with parsley, and serve.

name: Cheesy Crab Rangoon Garlic Bread
steps: Mix crab, cream cheese, and seasonings of choice into a filling.
Spread butter on the halved bread, then the crab filling, then mozzarella.
Bake at 400F for 25-30 minutes until golden and bubbly.

ingredients_full.note: tsp, optional but authentic to the original

steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 400F.

In a medium bowl, combine the imitation crab, cream cheese, 30g green onion, soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, 1 tsp of the garlic powder, red pepper flakes, white pepper, and MSG if using. Mix until smooth and well combined - this is the crab rangoon filling.
In a separate bowl, mix the softened butter with parsley, Parmesan, minced garlic, and the remaining 1 tsp garlic powder until fully combined.
Place the halved loaf cut-side up on a baking sheet. Spread the garlic butter evenly over both halves, all the way to the edges.
Spread the crab filling evenly over the garlic butter, then top generously with the shredded mozzarella.
Bake 25-30 minutes, until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and lightly golden in spots.
As soon as it comes out of the oven, brush the top with sweet chili sauce and finish with a generous sprinkle of extra chopped green onion.
Slice and serve warm - this is meant to be shared.

name: French Toast (Protein-Boosted)
steps: Whisk egg, milk, and cinnamon.
Dip bread and cook in a pan 2-3 min per side.
steps_detailed: Whisk the egg, egg whites, milk, cinnamon, and vanilla together in a shallow dish.
Dip each bread slice in the mixture, letting it soak 10-15 seconds per side so the custard penetrates rather than just coating the surface.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Cook the bread 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through in the center.
